entry.title: SuccessFactors Release 1H 2026, Important notes on EC, Platform, Time Management, my view
entry.content:
First thing First 😊. Screenshots have been taken from SAP release notes only.

Latest Home Page Is Now Automatically On by Default (WEF-167105) – As per 1H 2026 release SAP has enabled the latest home page experience automatically in all systems. In case if you don’t want to adopt the latest home page make sure after release hits your production system you are turning it off in the configuration.

Deprecation of Legacy Home Page Experience (WEF-166700) – Legacy Home will be deleted on Nov 13, 2026. Customer must plan accordingly to use the latest home page. Change Management team should communicate to the end users about that. In case if you are using automated test scripts then you need plan to update the test scripts as the screen is getting changed.

Deprecation of Legacy People Profile (SFEDU-21006)– Legacy people profile will be deleted on May 15, 2026. In the meantime, latest people profile will be universally available for all customers and does not require an enablement setting. As per my testing I can see there is no major issue in the latest people profile but for Time Off Solution if the “Time Account” object is unsecured then you have to adjust a bit the configuration of Time Account Object & few changes in RBP. SAP KBA for that KBA - 3625336.

Improvements to Enhanced People Search (ISH-28758) – It’s cool. User status can be used in the filter criteria. To filter, enter keywords such as inactive David or Active David. Also, for national id You can enter the asterisk (*) as a wildcard in the National ID field for the search criteria.

Enhancements to Editing UI of Full Profile (ECT-263295) – Few screens have been modified for latest people profile along with some error message layouts have been updated.

Important observation - There is no pie chart in Compensation Information.

 

Subheading Names for the Message Popup 

Direct Workflow Approval Link in Manage Data Page (MDF-89756) – In the Manage data page workflow approval link is available which helps streamlining the process of reviewing pending or completed workflows.

Auto-Reassigning Primary Phone and Email Records (ECT-254499) - After enabling the auto-reassignment option, when you import a new primary record, the system automatically updates the existing primary record to non-primary. The import is successful and a warning message is displayed.

Old version: It showed error message.

Automated Deletion of Future-Dated Job and Compensation Information Records After Employment Termination (ECT-260432) – If this feature is enabled then all future-dated Job Information records with the relevant event reason set to delete are automatically removed during the Terminate action, regardless of how the records were created.

In the earlier version, even after the termination the future dated Job & Compensation Information records remained in the system after the termination which required manual deletion.

Workflow Participant Information Available in Mobile Apps (MOB-107874) - Cool one. In the mobile app user can view the with whom the request is pending for approval. The feature applies to Employee Central, MDF, Time Off, and Time Sheet workflows.

 

 

Enhancements to Time Off and Time Sheet Custom Workflow Notifications (TIM-52812) – Nice one. Custom workflow notification text can be used for Time Off, Time Sheet and Single Record Approvals through the document generation mechanism. Following dynamic fields are supported.

  • Employee name
  • Time type recorded by the employee
  • Start Time/Start Date and End Time/End Date of the absence or attendance request

entry.title: Variance calculation without Target cost
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am trying to run variance calculation for a production order. Since we do not have a standard cost estimate for this article, there is no target cost in the production order. Therefore, I am expecting only remaining variance in the variance calculation. During the KKS2 run, the system shows the warning message “KV 151: Only remaining variance in version 0.” However, the system is still showing resource usage variance and output price variance. Does anyone know how the system is able to calculate these variance categories when no target cost exists in the production order?

entry.title: Re: RC - EC integration
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible. Make sure you use the same picklist for this custom field in EC. If the field is not showing in mapping, then it can be because of 2 issues 

1. Check if you are using same data type in both RCM and EC.

2. Make sure the field is reportable if you are using custom field in RCM.

entry.title: Stock Allocation from Multiple MRP Area
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

We have two MRP areas within same plant. One for sales processing with Plant 1000 as MRP area and Second MRP area (Plant 1000 & Location : XYDM) for Production orders where business can run MRP for independent requirements and continuously manufacture Make To Stock Products. Business will transfer the stock from XYDM location to other storage locations within 1000 plant regularly. 

During Sales order creation, availability will be performed within MRP area 1000 & stock available other than XYDM storage location will be reserved.  Business wants the automatic stock allocation from other MRP area also if stock not available in the main MRP area. The Products are batch managed. Recommended the customer to add the storage location in the Sales order so that the stock from XYDM location can be considered. Business team not accepting manual intervention and expecting auto allocation.

Customer using S4 HANA Public Cloud system and Basic ATP enabled. Customer not ready to spend for aATP.

entry.title: After moving to RISE with SAP, what changed the most in your operating model or delivery approach?
entry.content:
Moving to RISE with SAP is often framed as a technical shift, but in practice, it tends to change how teams actually work.

Things like ownership, decision-making, and even how delivery is structured don’t always stay the same. In some cases, processes become more standardized; in others, teams need to adapt in ways they didn’t fully expect.

If you’ve gone through that transition, what changed the most for you in your operating model or delivery approach?

entry.title: Customizing GRC NWBC screens globally without PFCG role update
entry.content:
Challenge: Fragmented GRC NWBC appearance and work center structure for different user groups in organisation leading to inconsistent UI layouts when accessing GRC functionality through NWBC, leading to reduced productivity and increased training costs. A uniform NWBC Landing page need to be established.

 

Implementation Approach: A uniform NWBC Landing page need to be configured which is applicable across all users accessing NWBC launch screen without changing roles.

In this blog, the detailed steps for configuring NWBC screens in highlighted.

Benefits

  • Eliminates Need for Technical ABAP Changes

Many minor UI adjustments, such as showing/hiding: Hyperlinks, Menu sections, Application nodes can be handled via customizing mode without involving developers

  • Helps Maintain Audit & Compliance Standards

Since NWBC is used by auditors, risk teams, and provisioning teams, controlling UI visibility helps ensure Least privilege navigation, No exposure of admin tools to business users, Clear segmentation of duties within the UI.

 

Environment: GRC Access Control 12.0

 

To achieve Centralized Control of UI Structure, GRC Admins can change what appears on NWBC for all users, from a single configuration point. No need to adjust individual roles repeatedly.

 In NWBC landing page, Right click -> option ‘Call Application in customizing mode’.

 

Segregation of Duties approach to prevent unintended customization:

Authorization object S_WDR_ADM controls the access to option ‘Call Application in customizing mode’ through which elements like visibility of each hyperlink/section can be globally changed for all users.

  • GRC Admins (Super user) or User designated to perform NWBC UI changes can be assigned with Authorization object S_WDR_ADM with full access in the PFCG role
  • For all other users, restrict Authorization object S_WDR_ADM to display only or disable it in the PFCG role.

 

Steps:

NOTE:  Exercise caution in applying these changes as it will be applicable to all users accessing NWBC irrespective of role assignment.

For Example - If there is a requirement that hyperlink ‘Work Inbox – Simplified’ should not be visible to any users who uses NWBC, then we can follow the below steps

1. Right Click on hyperlink ‘Work Inbox – Simplified’ -> Call Application in Customizing mode

2. Choose the Transport Request

3. Right click -> Call Application in Classic Customizing Mode

4. Right click -> Settings for Current Configuration

5. Set the Visibility to Invisible. 

6. After the changes are applied, ‘Work Inbox – Simplified’ hyperlink is not visible. You can always switch it back to default by following the same previous steps

 

You can follow the same steps to customise other NWBC screen hyperlinks or buttons.

 

Conclusion:

You have now successfully customised the GRC NWBC screen to display only required hyperlinks for all users.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for Mining Industry - SAP S/4HANA Publ...
entry.content:
Dear @melikegenisler,

This recommendation is tailored to the Mining industry from a project-approach perspective. SAP current Public Cloud positioning for mining is based on standard best-practice processes and industry guidance, not on a separate mining-specific scope package or dedicated scope items strictly for the Mining industry. Therefore the safest approach is to start from the core standard scope in SAP Signavio Process Navigator and then assess mining-specific requirements separately

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-end-to-end-business-processes-in-sap-business-suite/navigating-the-sap-best-practice-solution-processes-via-sap-signavio-process-navigator

As a starting point I would recommend focusing first on:

  • Record-to-Report / finance foundation: J58 Accounting and Financial Close; optionally 1GA Accounting and Financial Close Group Ledger IFRS if an additional IFRS ledger is required
  • Procure-to-Pay: J45 Procurement of Direct Materials, BNX Consumable Purchasing and 22Z Procurement of Services
  • Enterprise Asset Management / maintenance: 4HH Reactive Maintenance and 4HI Proactive Maintenance if relevant also 43R Maintenance Resource Scheduling
  • Projects: 1NT Project Control for Finance supported by 1YF Review a Financial Project
  • Inventory and warehouse visibility: 3BR Warehouse Inbound Processing and 3BS Warehouse Outbound Processing

After that mining-specific gaps such as weighbridge integration, commodity/commercial requirements, transport integration, lab/quality or site-specific field operations should be assessed separately and where needed addressed through clean-core extensions or partner solutions rather than ERP-heavy modification

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Unified Role-Based Compliance & Skills: Bridging the Gap Between Learning and Talent Intelligence Hu
entry.content:
In highly regulated industries compliance isn't just a "nice-to-have"; it is the backbone of operations. However, as we transition into the era of the Skills-Based Organization, many of us are encountering a significant architectural hurdle: How do we create a single source of truth for qualifications that satisfies both the Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) and the rigorous tracking of SuccessFactors Learning (LMS)?

I am currently working on a scenario where the goal is to harmonize job-specific qualifications, recurring certifications, and the new Growth Portfolio.

The Business Challenge: Fragmented Data & Administrative Overhead
Currently, many organizations face a "double maintenance" trap. Job requirements are defined in the Job Profile Builder (JPB), while the actual mandatory training and re-certification logic (e.g., annual safety instructions) live within the LMS.

This creates several pain points:

Inconsistent Data: A "skill" in TIH might not reflect a "certification" status in Learning.

Manual Mapping: Administrators must manually ensure that a specific job role is assigned the correct curricula in the LMS.

Lack of Transparency: Managers often have to jump between the Growth Portfolio and LMS Reports to see if their team is truly "ready to work."

Our Proposed Solution Concept
To solve this, we are looking at a framework that centers on the Job Architecture as the driver for compliance:

Job Profile Builder as the Anchor: Define all role-specific requirements (one-time and recurring) directly within the Job Role.

TIH as the Central Hub: Use the Talent Intelligence Hub to aggregate these attributes. We want to see "Certificates" as a specific attribute type that mirrors LMS completion statuses.

Automated Sync: Establishing a bidirectional flow where an LMS completion automatically updates the Growth Portfolio and validates the requirement in the Job Profile.

Centralized Reporting: Utilizing the Report Center to build dashboards that merge TIH skill data with LMS compliance deadlines.

The "Golden Grail": Role-Based Automation
The ultimate goal is to reduce administrative effort. Imagine a world where you don't assign a "Safety Training" element in the LMS, but rather define a "Safety Requirement" on the Job Role. SuccessFactors would then automatically trigger the corresponding Learning Path and update the employee's Skill Profile upon completion.

Questions to the Community | Seeking Best Practices
We believe this integration is crucial for the long-term adoption of the Talent Intelligence Hub. However, we would love to hear from other implementation partners and customers:

How are you handling recurring compliance? Are you using TIH "Attributes" to track validity periods, or are you keeping that logic strictly within the LMS?

Data Consistency: Have you found a sustainable way to prevent "data silos" between the Growth Portfolio and the LMS history?

Talent Intelligence Hub Mapping: Which attribute types are you using in TIH to represent formal licenses versus soft skills?

Does this resonate with your project? We believe many companies would benefit from a more native, role-based compliance logic within the SF Suite. If you have implemented a similar "integrated" approach or have thoughts on the upcoming SAP roadmap regarding this, let's start a conversation in the comments!

entry.title: First Half 2026 Release: Agentic AI in SAP SuccessFactors Learning
entry.content:
At SAP SuccessFactors, we’re focused on turning learning, career, and talent development into a strategic engine that helps people grow where business needs them most. By embedding skills-first development and data-driven talent planning into the flow of work, we’re making upskilling continuous, actionable, and aligned to future demands—so employees progress faster and leaders can plan with confidence.

We are continuing to deliver innovations to create dynamic upskilling and continuous learning that supports growth and adaptability; build future-ready, agile skill capabilities that evolve with business needs; and enable strategic talent pipelines powered by predictive, data-driven development insights.

In 2025 we made learning, job, and opportunity recommendations more contextual and actionable. In 2026 we’re delivering several new enhancements that will help bring those capabilities to life with conversational and agent-driven experiences.

Learning with Intelligent Q&A

Learning with Intelligent Q&A allows users to utilize Joule to summarize content and link back to source material - providing employees quick answers and guided next steps without losing context. This new innovation enables your teams with greater efficiency in discovering the right answers to questions, a reduced need to navigate through an entire course to find the right reference, and improved individual and organizational learning outcomes; all of which greatly improve the employee experience.

Learning with Intelligent Q&A on the learning homepage

Learning Compliance Agent

The new Learning compliance agent uses AI to help support users to get their training completed on time, and assists managers with follow-up, reducing the administrative burden so employees and managers can focus on more strategic tasks. With AI-assisted nudges, users will stay compliant with guidance at every step as Joule will proactively monitor users’ compliance status, always ready to help them get back on track.

Learning Compliance agent on the learning homepage

Together, these updates reduce skill gaps, increase internal mobility, and make talent planning proactive rather than reactive—helping organizations keep pace with change while employees see clearer paths to growth.

Learning Insights in People Intelligence

Learning Insights dashboard page in People Intelligence

People Intelligence capabilities in SAP SuccessFactors Learning help organizations to understand learning impact and create a future-ready workforce by providing actionable insights into employee training, development, and skills acquisition across the organization.

Learning insights allows leaders to:

  • Identify Training Participation Trends: Tracks how many employees engage with learning activities and highlights demographic patterns, such as age groups most involved in training.
  • Measure Training Effectiveness: Assesses skills growth and the impact of training on key performance indicators like promotion rates and performance ratings.
  • Optimize Learning Investments: Pinpoints which courses are most popular and effective, guiding resource allocation toward high-impact learning programs.
  • Detect Skill Gaps: Reveals areas or groups with low training hours or limited skills growth, helping the business target future development efforts more effectively.
  • Support Compliance and Development Goals: Ensures training activities align with organizational goals for employee development, compliance, and workforce readiness.

Continuously Delivering Enhancements     

With every release, we are excited to deliver these new innovations and continue our journey of providing even greater value to our customers. For full details, please check out the 1H 2026 Release Highlights Resources: 

  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Brochure
  • 1H 2026 Release Highlights Video 
  • Product Release & Road Map Information

Learn more about SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

entry.title: Re: Hierarchy upload from S/4 publich cloud to BW4H
entry.content:
Hello Guys,
we faced the same issues and finally got a solution at the end.
 
In Advance some information:
  • we solved it for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. I cannot guarantee that it works for Public Edition as well!
  • we are using own InfoObject in BW, no 0GL_ACCOUNT etc
  • we are using Z-CDS Views, but those are using SAP CDS Views (names down below and in the mentioned blog)
 
Technical Steps:
1) Changing Data Model and InfoObject
First of all, we changed our own InfoObject for GL_Account plus the data model like described in the following blog (it is about Costcenter, but also worked for GL_Account):
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cds-based-data-extraction-part-iii-miscellaneous/ba-p/13452148
 
Some steps of the blog has to be adapt due to the fact that we loading GL_Account instead of Costcenter:
For the Hierarchy Node Text we used CDS View: I_GLAccountHierarchyNodeT
But we enhanced this CDS View with an association to table HRRP_NODET_N to add field KTOPL (Charts of Account). The Charts of Account is compounded to IO GL_Account in BW.
That means that we also compounded the extra InfoObject for the Hierarchy Node Text the blog writes about to the Charts of Account as well.
 
2) Implenting coding for Hierarchy-TRNF DS -> IO GL_Account
After all that we still had the issue Tom described.
His coding was a very good idea but it needs to be adjusted:
  • it needs to be in the startroutine
  • it has to write back the Fieldnames instead of the IO-names for Segment 0003
Like this:
    LOOP AT source_package_3 ASSIGNING <source_fields_3>.
      IF <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'HIERARCHYNODE'.
        <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'HIERARCHYNODE'.
      ELSE.
        <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'GLACCOUNT'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
If you are using the SAP CDS View I_GLAccountHierarchyNode (SQL View: IFIGLACCOUNTHN) for the hierarchy extraction, the coding should fit for you. If not you might have diffrent field names. Basically you have adress the coding to the fields which are used in the CDS View from table HRRP_NODE_N and fields HRYNODE + SAKNR.
 
3) Adjust rule in Hierarchy-TRNF DS -> IO GL_Account in Tab "Hierarchy Structure" (Segment 0003)
  • set the mapping of FIELDNM to OH_IOBJNM to Hierarchy Derivation
 
=== DONE ===
 
After all this the data load finally worked and the hierarchy in the query looks fine 🙂
 
Hope I didnt forget any important step and this helps to any who faces similar issues...

entry.title: Re: Hierarchy upload from S/4 publich cloud to BW4H
entry.content:
Hello Guys,
we faced the same issues and finally got a solution at the end.
 
In Advance some information:
  • we solved it for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. I cannot guarantee that it works for Public Edition as well!
  • we are using own InfoObject in BW, no 0GL_ACCOUNT etc
  • we are using Z-CDS Views, but those are using SAP CDS Views (names down below and in the mentioned blog)
 
Technical Steps:
1) Changing Data Model and InfoObject
First of all, we changed our own InfoObject for GL_Account plus the data model like described in the following blog (it is about Costcenter, but also worked for GL_Account):
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/cds-based-data-extraction-part-iii-miscellaneous/ba-p/13452148
 
Some steps of the blog has to be adapt due to the fact that we loading GL_Account instead of Costcenter:
For the Hierarchy Node Text we used CDS View: I_GLAccountHierarchyNodeT
But we enhanced this CDS View with an association to table HRRP_NODET_N to add field KTOPL (Charts of Account). The Charts of Account is compounded to IO GL_Account in BW.
That means that we also compounded the extra InfoObject for the Hierarchy Node Text the blog writes about to the Charts of Account as well.
 
2) Implenting coding for Hierarchy-TRNF DS -> IO GL_Account
After all that we still had the issue Tom described.
His coding was a very good idea but it needs to be adjusted:
  • it needs to be in the startroutine
  • it has to write back the Fieldnames instead of the IO-names for Segment 0003
Like this:
    LOOP AT source_package_3 ASSIGNING <source_fields_3>.
      IF <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'HIERARCHYNODE'.
        <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'HIERARCHYNODE'.
      ELSE.
        <source_fields_3>-fieldnm = 'GLACCOUNT'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
If you are using the SAP CDS View I_GLAccountHierarchyNode (SQL View: IFIGLACCOUNTHN) for the hierarchy extraction, the coding should fit for you. If not you might have diffrent field names. Basically you have adress the coding to the fields which are used in the CDS View from table HRRP_NODE_N and fields HRYNODE + SAKNR.
 
3) Adjust rule in Hierarchy-TRNF DS -> IO GL_Account in Tab "Hierarchy Structure" (Segment 0003)
  • set the mapping of FIELDNM to OH_IOBJNM to Hierarchy Derivation
 
=== DONE ===
 
After all this the data load finally worked and the hierarchy in the query looks fine 🙂
 
Hope I didnt forget any important step and this helps to any who faces similar issues...

Kind regards,
Arnold

entry.title: Integration capabilities highly rated in SAP BTP reviews
entry.content:
Only a few short months ago I posted a blog in celebration of SAP Business Technology Platform's (SAP BTP) performance in G2’s Winter 2026 awards for their Cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS) category. Now having quickly accelerated to their Spring 2026 awards, once again I am happy to report that SAP BTP featured prominently, achieving leader badges along with a leaders position in the G2 Enterprise Grid for CPaaS.  

 Connections count!

Reading just a handful of the most recent SAP BTP reviews on the G2 website reveals just how important robust yet flexible integration is to SAP customers. Among the most recent customer reviews, many reveal Integration as something for which they rate SAP BTP highly:

  • 18 April 2026 I use SAP Business Technology Platform for data integration, analytics, and app development. It connects multiple systems, reduces data silos, and streamlines workflows, providing real-time insights. Its scalability handles large data efficiently. 
  • 16 April 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps address the challenge of integrating, extending, and modernising SAP landscapes by offering a unified platform for integration, analytics, data management, and application development. For us, this means simpler system integration, better visibility through analytics, and faster innovation without disrupting our core SAP systems. 
  • 10 April 2026 By utilizing the SAP Integration Suite, we successfully automated the data flow between our core ERP and third-party tools. The biggest benefit is the dramatic reduction in manual IT maintenance, saving us countless hours each month. 
  • 16 March 2026 SAP Business Technology Platform helps us connect systems, manage data, and build extensions for SAP apps without changing the core system, keeping it stable. It speeds up development by allowing integration and new functions, ensuring easy modifications without risking the main setup. 
  • 12 February 2026  SAP technology primarily solves the problem of disconnected, inefficient, and siloed business processes within organizations. By acting as a "central nervous system," SAP integrates data across finance.         

And this is just a small sample of the reviews available on G2 – there are many more that speak about the benefits of SAP BTP not only for integration, but application development, automation and support for clean core development too.

Springing forward with new badges

Having secured our Spring 2026 badges from G2, what next for SAP BTP? Obviously, Summer 2026 badges are now in view, and with support from our fabulous customers we shall hope to achieve these next. But first for the SAP BTP team, along with others from SAP, will be a focus on our very own Sapphire 2026 events, due to take place in Orlando and Madrid next month. The SAP BTP team will be presenting at both of these prestigious events, so if you happen to be joining one or the other, please make sure to come and say hello to the team on the show floor. And if you’re unable to join either of these events in-person, then that’s no problem, instead please join us at the SAP Sapphire Virtual event.

 With that I’ll sign off for this time, but meanwhile don’t forget to go to the G2 website and read more reviews for the customer-viewpoint on SAP BTP!

Chris Grundy, Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: Re: Hierarchy upload from S/4 publich cloud to BW4H
entry.content: Hello Cristina, we have the same issue, segment 4 is empty. entry.title: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2026: Getting past the AI skills gap with help from your ASUG peers
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is about connection. One of SAP’s goals for the event is to connect its customers with the right solution to solve their business needs. The keynotes, customer demos, and product showcases focus on this. Another key objective is bringing people together - customers, partners, and SAP experts - to have meaningful conversations about problems enterprises are facing and how to address them. The sessions in ASUG’s Community Hub provide a structured framework to tackle complex topics such as how to adopt AI in your organization.

How are you feeling about AI in your workplace? Answer in the chat below.

Why we care about AI and adoption

Nicole Smythe and I are facilitating an ASUG Community Conversation on how to overcome barriers to AI implementation in your organization (ERP1864). Our goal is to help participants create a 90-day plan to turn your thoughts into action and move forward with AI in your organizations.

Nicole leads product marketing for AI in SAP Cloud ERP Private, so she is very focused on ensuring that customers obtain the maximum value from their investments in SAP. My team, Cloud ERP UX Foundation, creates embedded AI capabilities for SAP Cloud ERP, so we want people to use what we build. I’m also active in ASUG (co-lead for SAP Cloud ERP community and Northern California chapter) and the SAP Community. We’re both excited to meet with customers to find out what is holding them back from going all-in on AI.

SAP has many options for AI: embedded and Joule, base and premium

SAP has many options for AI, but which is best for you depends on your organizational readiness. Organizational readiness is not based on what top executives say about using AI, it’s about how individual people use AI to make their jobs more efficient. And usage only happens if people have the knowledge about the AI tools and technologies and how to use them effectively in their specific roles.

SAP has a range of AI solutions to address different business challenges.

In our various interactions with customers, we are finding a range of feelings from optimism to fear about using AI in their jobs. Sometimes we see a mix of feelings in the same person. Yes, AI can be very confusing. One thing that most people agree on is that they are not confident their organizations have the skills to succeed with AI.

Bridging the AI skills gap starts with identifying the specific challenges

That is the purpose of our ASUG workshop. We want to address the skills gap and get people talking about what barriers they need to overcome to implement AI in their organizations. We’ll split into groups and people can discuss what skills they feel they are missing and brainstorm ideas on how to overcome one or two challenges.

If you are going to Sapphire and are thinking about rolling out AI in your organization, please join us to learn some ideas about how to accelerate your progress. If you have already figured this out, please join us to share your knowledge with your peers.

To help us prepare for what’s important to you, please comment below.

  • How are you feeling about AI in your workplace?
  • Where do you think AI will have the biggest impact?
  • How are you measuring the impact of AI in your organization?
  • What skills do you think are most important for successful AI usage?
  • What skills are missing from your organization that keeps you from mass adoption?

The SAP Community is another safe space to have these conversations through a combination of learning, coaching, and engaging with peers. Please join us online at https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/business-ai  to talk about AI and how it can make your work life better.  Follow the AI group to learn more about what SAP is doing.

See you at Sapphire!

entry.title: Re: Oauth2 grant_type ''password" is not working after JD...
entry.content:
As part of the JDK 21 framework update, SAP has modernized the OAuth implementation by replacing the legacy Spring Security OAuth library (which reached end of life) with Spring Authorization Server. With this change, certain OAuth flows that are considered insecure or deprecated by current standards are no longer supported, including:

  • Resource Owner Password Credentials Grant (grant_type=password)
  • Implicit grant

As a result, requests using grant_type=password will return unsupported_grant_type, as observed in your curl example. This is the intended behavior and there is no SAP patch or configuration to re‑enable this grant type

SAP guidance recommends switching to one of the supported OAuth flows depending on the use case: 

  • Authorization Code Flow (with PKCE) for user‑based authentication (storefronts, UIs, mobile apps)
  • Client Credentials Flow for system‑to‑system or technical integrations

To help you navigate this change, you can follow the next guides: Framework Updates, Moving to the New OAuth Implementation, Composable Storefront local setup and OAuth Framework Update JDK21 

 💡  Have questions or feedback on this content?

Explore the SAP Customer Experience Value Map, which combines expert-led guidance with self-paced learning materials:

  • Blogs and Articles – Stay up to date with the latest enablement stories, tips, and insights from SAP experts.
  • Event Calendar – Join live sessions, webinars, and workshops designed to help you learn directly from specialists.
  • Best Practices and Guides – Access ready-to-use materials that simplify complex CX topics.
  • Continuous Quality Check (CQC) Services – Keep your SAP CX systems running efficiently.

entry.title: A small config change that saved my HR users a lot of headache — Fuzzy Search in SuccessFactors EC
entry.content:
Introduction

During one of my EC implementations in SAU project, I kept hearing the same complaint from HR users: they couldn't find employees through global search. A small issue on the surface, but it was slowing down daily work for the entire HR team. This post covers what was causing it and the one setting that fixed it.

What was happening

We were working with a client that had a large Arabic-speaking workforce and one thing you quickly notice in that context is that Arabic names transliterated into English don't have one fixed spelling. Mohammed, Muhammad, Mohamed all the same name, all spelled differently depending on who filled in the form.

HR users were complaining that they couldn't find employees through global search. My first reaction honestly was are they just not typing the name correctly? But after connected with a few of them and watching how they searched, I realized the problem wasn't them at all.

They were doing things like:

  • Typing Muhamed instead of Mohammed
  • Searching with a partial name like Fatim instead of Fatima
  • Small typos like Khaleed instead of Khalid 

and in all these cases the system just returned nothing, or completely irrelevant results. So they'd try again, rephrase, try again and wasting time on something that should take 5 seconds.

The fix — enabling Fuzzy Search

Fuzzy Search allows the system to return close matches even when the input is not exact. It handles spelling variations, partial names, and minor typos. Once I enabled it, the results changed immediately.

 

 

Before:

✗Muhamed → no results
✗Fatim → nothing useful
✗Khaleed → search fails

After:

✓Muhamed → suggests Mohammed
✓Fatim → returns Fatima
✓Khaleed → suggests Khalid

How to enable it

Simple Admin Center config — took me less than 2 minutes:

  1. Go to Admin Center
  2. Click on Tools
  3. Search for Company System and Logo Settings
  4. Enable "Fuzzy Search for People Search"

 Save Company System Settings

⏱Give it about 10 minutes to kick in

A few practical notes

One thing I noticed during testing Fuzzy Search works specifically in the Global Header People Search. It is not a system wide setting, so do not expect it to fix search issues in People Profile or the Employee Directory. Those areas work differently and have their own separate permissions and settings. If users are still complaining about search in those areas after you enable this, that is a separate investigation.

 

Conclusion

This experience was a good reminder that not every user complaint points to a training gap. Sometimes, the system simply is not configured to match real world usage.

In regions like Saudi Arabia, where Arabic name transliterations can vary significantly, enabling Fuzzy Search is not just a nice to have. It is practically essential for effective People Search.

If you are implementing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central for a multilingual or Arabic speaking workforce and have not reviewed this setting yet, it is definitely worth checking. It is a quick, 2 minute configuration change that can greatly improve everyday usability.

And the next time a search issue is raised, take a moment to review the system configuration before assuming it is a user error. It might just save you from drawing the wrong conclusion.

entry.title: Building Your Simple Business Roles with the IAM Apps
entry.content:
Introduction

In my blog Precise Control of Fiori App Access with the App Authorization Variants, I discussed newly available IAM Apps for the App Authorization Variant type (too wordy and hard to read! I call it IAM Apps or AAVs in this blog interchangeably if we confine the discussion in this context) to precisely control the Fiori app access from Release 2502.  This approach still performs authorization under the big umbrella of the Business Catalogs.

In this blog, I am going to discuss a new approach with an example: creating your simple business roles with the IAM Apps without using business catalogs. 

Background

I use a very simple case to illustrate the key point here.  Below figure shows a typical authorization case of one Business Role provides access to three Fiori Apps via one Business Catalog.

One Business Role Controls Three Apps through One Business Catalog

 For example, by exploring the Business Catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_AP_DSP_PC in a 2602 Release system, we find out it controls the access to three applications (see the red box below).  The reason is to form a semantically meaningful bundle of apps and app functions aligned to specific tasks or sub-processes. In a layman’s term, the Accounts Payable Configurator needs to access all these three applications to do the job. That is the intention we introduced and designed the concept of Business Catalogs for the SAP ERP Cloud.

The Business Catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_AP_DSP_PC Controls the Access to Three Apps

 

If we peel the onion with IAM App ID, we come out a new picture as shown below. Here we can observe the following:

  • App 1 has three IAM Apps IDs, A, B and C; each controls a specific transaction (remember, an app can contain one or more transactions as discussed in my previous blog)
  • App 2 has only one IAM Apps ID or associated with one transaction.
  • App 3 has no IAM Apps ID assigned, that makes App 3 always available if the Business Catalog is present.

One Business Role Controls Three Apps through One Business Catalog but with a Twist of IAM Apps

Still using the same Business Catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_AP_DSP_PC as an example, it can access three apps through each individual IAM App ID as highlighted in the above green box. It is simpler than my abstract illustration.

In the Authorization Model discussion of the SAP Help Portal, it mentions that IAM Apps ID can be assigned directly to a business role:

IAM apps can either be assigned directly to a business role or through a business catalog that bundles several IAM apps. The option to assign IAM apps directly is particularly useful when a business role requires only a few IAM apps from a specific business catalog. This approach helps you keep business roles lean without adding unnecessary business catalog content. However, if business catalogs represent tasks or sub-processes within a business process, it's best to continue assigning them to business roles. Then, activate only the IAM apps that are needed.

This means we can link the Business Role with Fiori Apps directly if there are IAM Apps as shown below.  In the case of App 3, we still need to use the Business Catalog to access it.

One Business Role Controls Three Apps through Four IAM Apps IDs and One Business Catalog

With this approach, we can effectively access App 1, run its embedded three transactions through three IAM Apps IDS, A, B and C.  The Business Catalog is no longer needed to access Apps 1-2, but the App 3.

While the IAM Apps approach provides an alternative way of creating a new Business Role, it is not advised to take this approach for all business roles as explained in above red text. It fits into simple use cases only.  Majority of SAP Business Role Templates are still Business Catalog based for good reasons.

Example

Let’s take a deep dive into one example, access the Fiori App Manage Business Users. From SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, we found out the following:

  • The App ID is F1303
  • There are five Business Catalogs associated with it

Five Business Catalogs Control Access to the Fiori App Manage Business Users

 Since I intend to go around the business catalog route to create a business role, I am going to ignore these five business catalogs but take the advantage of App ID F1303 directly.  I create a business role Z_USER_MGMT from scratch. 

An Empty User Defined Business Role Z_USER_MGMT

In the IAM Apps Tab, I use F1303 App ID to do a search.  It returns three unique IAM App IDs corresponding to exactly five Business Catalogs listed in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library. They are

  • F1303_03_TRAN                    for Displaying Business Users
  • F1303_22_TRAN                    for Assigning Business Users to Business Roles
  • F1303_TRAN                            for Maintaining Business Users

Three IAM App IDs associated with the App ID F1303

I select these three IAM App IDs to be part of my newly created user role Z_USER_MGMT. 

The User Role Z_USER_MGMT with three IAM App IDS

To ease the role consumption in the Fiori Launchpad, I assign the standard Launchpad Space SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR to the role.

The User Role Z_USER_MGMT with the standard Launchpad Space SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR

Last thing to do is assigning my test user testy as a business user to this role.  Save the role to update the role authorization.

A Complete User Role Z_USER_MGMT without Any Business Catalogs

In the Google Incognito window, I logged in as the user testy.  Since this user only has one role Z_USER_MGMT assigned, there is only one Page Identity and Access Management inherited from the Space SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR is shown.

My Home Page of the User testy

By clicking on the Administration tab next to My Home, or the Identity and Access Management Page, we can see the only Fiori App Maintain Business Users is available. All other spaces, pages and Fiori apps are unavailable due to limited authorizations.

After opening the app Maintain Business Users, the Edit button is grayed out. Why?

Cannot Edit a User in Maintain Business User app

I scratched my head for two days and finally was told by my colleague that there is one secret when creating a new business role: by default, the Write, Read, Value Help Access Category is marked as No Access. That prohibits users to make changes in the app.  I overlooked at this.

No Access Default Setting for the Access Category: Write, Read, Value Help

After selecting the right value of the Write, Read, Value Help Access Category, I can manage any users the same way as we have the SAP_BR_ADMINISTRATOR business role.  To further illustrate the effectiveness of these IAM Apps IDs, I removed F1303_22_TRAN IAM Apps ID. Now I cannot maintain the business roles as both the Add and Remove buttons are grayed out.

Add and Remove Buttons Are Grayed Out when IAM Apps ID F1303_22_TRAN Is Removed

Conclusion

This blog introduced a new approach of creating a simple Business Role by using the IAM Apps without Business Catalogs.  This approach suits the business scenario for a simple and straightforward business role. For a complicated scenario, especially a persona-based business role, using the Business Catalogs along with the IAM Apps serves better.

References

  • Blog: Precise Control of Fiori App Access with the App Authorization Variants
  • Blog: User Management in a Nutshell for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
  • SAP Help: Work with IAM Apps (App Authorization Variants)
  • SAP Help: Authorization Model

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi, good day! @noravonthenen here the Output cell from week 1... Thanks! Excellent start!

"Die Tafel" (literally "The Table") is the common name for a network of **food banks** in Germany. It is a crucial social initiative that plays a significant role in combating food waste and supporting people in need.

Here's a breakdown of what "die Tafel" is:

1. **Purpose:**
* **Combating Food Waste:** They collect surplus, perfectly edible food that would otherwise be thrown away by
supermarkets, bakeries, manufacturers, and other donors.
* **Providing Food Assistance:** They distribute this collected food to individuals and families who are experiencing financial hardship.

2. **How it Works:**
* **Collection:** Volunteers pick up food items that are still good but can no longer be sold (e
.g., due to approaching best-before dates, damaged packaging, or overproduction).
* **Distribution:** The food is then sorted and distributed at various Tafel locations (often called "Tafel-Ausgabestellen" or "Tafel-Läden") to registered clients.
*
**Symbolic Fee:** Clients usually pay a very small, symbolic fee (e.g., 1 or 2 Euros) for a bag of groceries, which helps cover some operational costs.

3. **Who it Helps:**
* Individuals and families who are experiencing poverty or financial hardship. This
includes:
* Recipients of social benefits (e.g., Bürgergeld, Wohngeld).
* Low-income earners.
* Pensioners with small pensions.
* Single parents.
* Homeless individuals.
* Refugees and asylum
seekers.
* To receive food from die Tafel, individuals usually need to provide proof of their financial need (e.g., benefit statements, income statements).

4. **Key Characteristics:**
* **Volunteer-Driven:** The vast majority of Tafel operations are run by dedicated volunteers who donate
their time to collect, sort, and distribute food.
* **Non-Profit:** Die Tafel organizations operate on a non-profit basis and rely heavily on donations (food, financial, and in-kind).
* **Nationwide Network:** There are over 960 Tafel locations across Germany,
making it a widespread and accessible support system.

5. **Significance:**
* Die Tafel provides essential food support, helping to alleviate hunger and improve the nutritional situation of many vulnerable people.
* It also raises awareness about food waste and promotes sustainable consumption.

If you or someone you
know needs assistance from die Tafel, it's best to visit their official website or contact your local Tafel directly to inquire about their specific eligibility requirements, registration process, and opening hours.

**Official Website:** [www.tafel.de](https://www.tafel.de)

entry.title: Re: Data persistence in Datasphere
entry.content:
Persistency will not open ASSOC. Assoc by definition are temporary and will be used by AM only. Not view persisting. 

It has to show 750k. Try to copy the view into a very new one. I have had the issue of obsolete version in runtime (I was deploying to PROD however) and I was reading such issues were not uncommon in DWC. What helped me was to copy the view... 

Try also without any associations if not sure. 

The inflation of 1:n View-Assoc happens in AM

entry.title: Blank PDF issue in SAP Fiori apps: Fix
entry.content:
Hi All

During my work with SAP UI5 applications, I newly faced an issue where PDF files won't open and instead displayed an empty screen in the browser which avoids users from accessing/ printing required documents. Upon analyzing, I identified that certain browser settings in Edge and Chrome were causing the issue.

Fix: 

To fix the blank PDF screen issue, I modified below settings in Edge and Chrome. These changes helped PDFs are managed to open within the browser environment.

Edge:

Chrome:

In the Site Settings, check if Pop-ups are allowed (if not, change the setting)

(old site settings screenshot)

After updating these changes, the PDF documents opened rightly without an empty screen. These settings assist the browser process and display PDF content from SAP UI5 apps.

The blank screen issue when opening PDFs in SAP UI5 applications can relate to browser configuration than the application itself. By adjusting the necessary settings in Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome, the issue can be fixed. Also, these changes are not limited to SAP UI5 and can be applied to other web applications facing similar PDF display issues.

Note: This blog post is originally written based on my personal experience and knowledge. I have ensured that the content reflects my own understanding.

Thank you!!
BR// Dhanasupriya Sidagam

entry.title: Re: workflow for manage recurring journal entries
entry.content:
Dear @MP_77,

There is no standard workflow/approval process for Manage Recurring Journal Entries. SAP states this explicitly workflow is available only for Verify General Journal Entries, not for recurring or reversing journal entries

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3430254

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3607875

The standard recurring-entry process is:

  1. Create the template in Manage Recurring Journal Entries
  2. Go to Schedule General Ledger Jobs
  3. Schedule the job Post Recurring Journal Entries
  4. Maintain the required selection parameters, including Due Date. SAP explains that recurring entries after the Due Date are not posted and the recommended setting is Due Date must cover the last planned posting date

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3035119

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3022472

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cd189f57e7356d10e10000000a441470.html

If the business requirement is approval before posting the standard SAP route is Verify General Journal Entries because SAP also confirms that workflow verification must be done through the Verify General Journal Entries suite, not through Post General Journal Entries and not through recurring entries

Summary:

For recurring journal entries in Public Cloud the process is create recurring entry, schedule Post Recurring Journal Entries job and monitor job result. A separate approval workflow for the recurring-entry object itself is not supported in standard

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Fiori/CAP – Draft enabled on entity but Edit/Delete not visible in Fiori elements object page
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m currently working with CAP OData V4 service used in a Fiori elements application.

I’m trying to enable editing (Edit button + Create/Delete for child entities) on the object page of an entity called Messages, but I’m running into issues with draft handling.

Current situation

I have enabled draft on the entity:

@odata.draft.enabled
entity Messages {
  key ID : UUID;
  // fields...
}

However, in the generated Fiori elements app for the Customer entity (from which I navigate to the Messages, as seen below in the Data model):

  • The Edit button is not visible on the Messages object page
  • Create/Delete is missing for its child composition

Data model (simplified)

entity Customer {
  supplyChains : Composition to many SupplyChains;
}

entity SupplyChains {
  messages : Association to many Messages;
}

@odata.draft.enabled
entity Messages {
  headers : Composition to many OtherEntity;
}

Assumption / Question

From my understanding, Fiori elements (OData V4) relies heavily on draft + composition hierarchies for enabling full CRUD functionality.

So I suspect the issue is caused by this part:

Association to many Messages

instead of a composition.

My questions

  1. Is a fully composition-based hierarchy required (Customer → SupplyChains → Messages) for draft editing to work properly in Fiori elements?
  2. If I keep the association between SupplyChains and Messages:
    • Is it still possible to enable Edit on the Messages object page?
    • Are there recommended patterns for this scenario?
  3. Do I need to enable @odata.draft.enabled on all parent entities, or only on Messages and its children?

Additional info

  • Service is exposed as OData V4
  • Fiori elements app generated in BAS
  • Messages is exposed via projection in the service

Any clarification on how draft + composition vs association affects UI editability in CAP would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition error message for i...
entry.content:
Dear @kanchane_jayasinghe36,

In Public Cloud SSCUI 102320 / Define Output Format of Material Numbers is the correct configuration activity, but changing the product/material number length from 18 to 40 characters is only possible after SAP activates Product Number Length Extension in the tenant. SAP Public Cloud documentation states that this activation is a prerequisite and only afterward can the new length be maintained in SSCUI 102320

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3560881

https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226/2502.06/en-US/WN_CE2502.1_EN.pdf

SAP Note 2696446 is consistent with that prerequisite. It explains what happens once the length setting is used including that the Material Number and Old Material Number are set with the same length and that the Old Material Number retains the highest length ever configured but it does not remove the need for SAP activation first. SAP also states that, once the length has been extended, it must not be reduced again

So based on the attached error the tenant is still validating against the currently effective shorter length so this should first be treated as an activation / tenant-status issue, not as a missing CBC configuration option. The recommended next step is for SAP to verify whether Product Number Length Extension is already active in the affected tenant. If it is not active, SAP needs to activate it first. If it is already active and SSCUI 102320 still raises BMG223 this should be handled by SAP as a tenant-specific support issue

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently setting up Joule Studio in SAP BTP and reviewing the prerequisites.

According to the documentation, Joule Studio requires an entitlement for SAP Build Process Automation with the service plan “build_default”.

However, in my subaccount I already have the “standard” (standard-application) plan assigned, which provides full production capabilities for SAP Build Process Automation (workflows, RPA, monitoring, etc.).

From my understanding:

  • The standard plan offers full functionality for productive use

  • The build_default plan seems to provide a more limited or base-level feature set

This raises a concern, as introducing or switching to build_default could potentially limit existing capabilities.

My questions are:

  1. Is the build_default plan technically required for Joule Studio, even if the standard plan is already available?

  2. Is there a dependency or integration requirement that specifically relies on build_default?

  3. Can both plans coexist without impacting existing Build Process Automation scenarios?

  4. What is the recommended entitlement setup for using Joule Studio in a productive BTP environment?

Any clarification or best practices would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order
entry.content:
1. I create 2 Sales Schedule Agreements with the same Customer, Material and Schedule Line Date

2. When I use app : Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference 

I can combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order 

3. But when I use app : Sales Order Schedule Lines

I can't combine multiple Sales Schedule Agreements into one Delivery Order and Customer is already flagged for the "Order Combination" in the Customer Master Data

Could you please help or suggest how to set up a combined Multiple Sales Schedule Agreement as one Delivery Order?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to set up combine Multiple Sales Schedule Agreeme...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Hope you are doing great.

We have identified that the behavior is related to how the app “Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery” processes delivery creation depending on the execution mode.

In this app, there are two different processing options:

1. Dialog (Create single delivery in dialog)

  • The system processes each selected document individually
  • As a result, only one outbound delivery per reference document is created
  • Combination of multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements is not supported in this mode

2. Background

  • The system processes documents via backend logic (function module RV_DELIVERY_CREATE)
  • In this case, multiple documents can be passed together
  • Therefore, order combination is supported, and a single outbound delivery can be created

This is standard system behavior:

  • Dialog -> no combination (one delivery per document)
  • Background -> combination possible

If your business scenario requires combining multiple Sales Scheduling Agreements into one delivery, we recommend using:

  • The Background option in Sales Order Schedule Lines – Due for Delivery, or
  • The app “Create Outbound Delivery with Order Reference”, which supports combination by design

Hope the above clarifies your queries.

Best regards,
Letícia

entry.title: Re: XML for Adobe Master template
entry.content:
A work around will be to put a break point within the CL_SOMU_FORM_SERVICES class.

That has to be done through the ADT tool. "Eclipse"

Within the method "get_document" put a breakpoint after  "__get_data( ".

Execute the output, when the debugger will open, select the variable ev_xml. It will contain the XML including the master form template section.

Make sure to remove the limitation of the variable display when the breakpoint stop.

Right click on the variable, select Show Detail as -> Default View

Right click in the view box, select "Max Length" and set it to "0" => illimited

Right click again on the variable "ev_xml", select "Show Detail as" -> XML view

Now you have the full XML including the Form master data.

entry.title: Re: ServiceNow being an external catalog for SAP Ariba Bu...
entry.content:
Ariba’s external catalog requirements are strict. A catalog must support:

  • OCI punchout (Open Catalog Interface)

  • Return of line-level data in OCI format

  • Supplier part number, description, price, UOM, UNSPSC

  • cXML wrapper compatibility

  • HTTPS endpoint with session handling

  • Ability to return a “cart” back to Ariba Guided Buying

  • No workflow or approval logic embedded in the external catalog

  • No dynamic pricing unless supported via OCI hooks

ServiceNow’s native catalog is designed for ITSM, not procurement. It does not ship with OCI punchout or cXML return capabilities by default.

ServiceNow Store App (Punchout Integration) — if licensed: Some customers use a ServiceNow Punchout plugin or partner-built connector that adds:

  • OCI outbound

  • OCI return

  • cXML envelope

  • Cart mapping

  • Session token handling

This makes ServiceNow behave like a compliant external catalog.

Note: ServiceNow is not an SAP-certified external catalog out of the box. However, it can function as one if the customer implements an OCI-compliant punchout integration that meets SAP Ariba’s external catalog requirements. Without this, Ariba Guided Buying remains the recommended and supported front-end for initiating IT service or item requests.

entry.title: Triggering SAP CPI iflow through SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is for highlighting the basics of using SAP Build Process Automation and how it can be leveraged in various business scenarios. For demo purpose, I have created SAP CPI iflow which gets triggered by using a Automation job created in SAP BPA.

 

  1. Create CPI iflow test_iflow as below to capture data from Northwind opensource odata services with HTTPs sender adapter- 

 2. Activate the CPI iflow - 

 3. Create Destination in SAP BTP account using oauth credentials (client-id, client-secret created in BTP account) 

 

4. Add the created Destination in SAP Build->Control_Tower as below-  

 

 5. Create ‘Project’ ztestingprj_iflow under Connectors->Actions as below-  

 

 

 

 6.

a.  Create ‘Action’ zjoule_action under project ‘ztestingprj_iflow’ with URL [/http/ztest_joule] from endpoint generated from CPI iflow ‘test_iflow’. 

b. In the ‘Output’ tab, we will capture only some part of the response payload in the Body as the result.

c. In Destination, select the destination which was created in BTP 

d. Click Test. The Action triggers CPI ilfow ‘test_iflow’ generating response [200 OK] with payload generated by CPI iflow.  

 

 7. Create ‘Process Automation’ project zProcess_prj under ‘Lobby’  

 

8. In Process Automation project created above; create Automation Artifact zauto1_sampleprocess as below- 

 

 9. In Automation Artifact created above, drag and drop the Action created in Step 6. 

 

 10. In ‘Project Properties’ create Environment Variable of type ‘destination’ as below and Save the  Automation Artifact- 

 11. To run the Automation in Desktop Agent create Automation Launcher zAutoLaunch1 as below- 

 

 12. In Automation Launcher created above drag and drop the Automation Artifact created in step 8. 

 13. Save the project. Release the required version and Deploy in ‘Public’ environment as below- 

 

 14. During Deployment, select the Destination created in BTP (step 3) in the Environment variable created in step 10.  

Click Deploy. 

 

 15. After successful Deployment the deployed version could be seen in the path as below- 

 16. Download Desktop Agent from SAP Development Tools as below and install in your system- 

 

 17. In Desktop Agent, click Add Tenant and register your SAP BPA account as below- 

 18. For Automation, set the Desktop Agent in ‘Unattended Mode’ as below- 

 

 19. After successful Tenant registration in Desktop Agent; we can see its entry in Agent Management under Control Tower path as below- 

 20. In ‘Triggers’ tab, create a trigger as per required schedule- 

 

 21. We can observe automatic triggering of the CPI iflow with the response payload- 

 22. In Monitoring->Automation Jobs; we can see the entries as per scheduled timings of trigger- 

 

 23. On Activating Traces, we can see the Traces showing response payloads coming from CPI iflow-

entry.title: Slicenceの仮（Temp）キーについて
entry.content:
DEVシステムログイン時、ライセンス有効期限にかかわるワーニングが表示されるため
Slicenceにて確認したところ、仮ライセンスの期限がもうすぐ終了するものの、永久ライセンスがすでに追加済でした。永久ライセンスのステータスはInactiveです。
この場合、仮ライセンスの削除が正しい操作でしょうか？
スクリーンショットにて、Slicenceの状況を共有させていただきます


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

１．Slicenceにてライセンスの情報を取得



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Slicenceの仮（Temp）キーについて
entry.content:
本事象について、以下の手順によって実施してください。

1. System 850853030にてHardware Key W0440932934で新しいキーを作成してください。

2. Slicenseですべての古いキーを削除してください。

3. Slicenseで新しいキーをインストールしてください。

そうすると、問題を解決するはずです。もしそれ以上の質問がある場合、遠慮なくお問い合わせください。

entry.title: What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR Keys application?
entry.content:
What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR Keys application?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: What are the required authorizations to use the SSCR...
entry.content:
To use the SSCR Keys application, your S-user ID must have the appropriate authorizations:
  • Register Object and Developer Keys
  • Register Object Keys

To request authorizations, use the Self-service Authorization Request - KBA 2891528

 To speed up processing for S-user ID authorization requests, I recommend contacting the existing administrators listed under Important Contacts within your company to approve your request.

For more detailed information, please refer to the KBAs below:

2453897 - How to request Object and/or Developer Keys (SSCR) for On-premise - SAP for Me

2489468 - Installation number is missing from the SSCR application - SAP for Me

2891528 - How to use the Self-Service Authorization Request - SAP for Me

How to speed up processing for S-user ID authorization requests
Contact your company's Administrators who are listed in Important Contacts.

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us.

Best regards,
SAP Support
